calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-21-2015 Off Calendar

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-18-2014 Continued To Feb-03-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-14-2014 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Dec-02-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-18-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Denied. Defendant City and County of San Francisco ("CCSF") has not met its initial burden of introducing evidence showing that Plaintiff cannot establish that the bus driver met his heightened duty of care, per Civ. Code sec. 2100. The only material facts presented in support of CCSF's contention that Plaintiff cannot establish breach are that the bus was being operated within the speed limit and that a car made an unexpected turn in front of the bus. (Declaration of Michael Irvin, pars. 4-6.) CCSF has provided no information about other facts relevant to whether the bus driver was acting within the heightened duty of care, such as whether the bus driver was paying careful attention to the road prior to the accident or how much time the bus driver had to react to the car turning in front of it. Thus, CCSF has not met its initial burden on summary judgment. (See Kramer v. Barnes (1963) 212 Cal.App.2d 440, 449, "In order to relieve himself of liability upon the basis that there was no triable issue of fact as to him, it was incumbent upon [the defendant] to show by facts, stated with the required particularity, that his conduct was in conformity with the standard and duties of care imposed upon him under the circumstances.") In any event, even if CCSF had met its initial burden, there are triable issues of material fact as to whether the bus driver met the heightened duty of care. The parties dispute whether the car unexpectedly turned in front of the bus, or whether the bus driver had sufficient warning that the car would move into the bus's lane. (Plaintiff's response to CCSF's Undisputed Material Facts 4-5.) "The question of whether the driver in the rear was negligent in colliding with the car in front is essentially a question of fact and not of law because it is dependent upon the circumstances of the individual case." (Id. at 447.) Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-12-2014 Continued To Sep-18-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

CROSS COMPLAINANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
